items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Sam Merrill Undergoes MRI On Hamstring
items.blurb: Sam Merrill Undergoes MRI On Hamstring — Cleveland Cavaliers guard Sam Merrill underwent an MRI on his left hamstring on Wednesday. Merrill, who was held out of practice, suffered the injury during the Cavs' Game 1 loss to the Pistons. Merrill averaged 12.8 points in the regular season. Game 2 takes place on Thursday.

items.headline: OG Anunoby Leaves Knicks' Game 2 Win With Leg Injury
items.blurb: OG Anunoby Leaves Knicks' Game 2 Win With Leg Injury — OG Anunoby left the New York Knicks' Game 2 win over the Philadelphia 76ers late in the fourth quarter due to an apparent leg injury. Anunoby walked gingerly off the court after attempting a dunk with 2:56 left in the game. The Knicks had no specific updates after the game. "It looked like he was hopping, but I don't know anything," coach Mike

items.headline: Jalen Brunson Scores 26 Points In Knicks' Game 2 Win Over 76ers
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson Scores 26 Points In Knicks' Game 2 Win Over 76ers — The New York Knicks defeated the Philadelphia 76ers 108-102 in Game 2 to take a 2-0 series lead on Wednesday night. The game featured 25 lead changes, the most for a playoff game in 11 years. Neither team lead by more than seven points in the game. items.headline: Jason Kidd's future could be in doubt after Mavericks hire Masai Ujiri
items.blurb: Jason Kidd's future could be in doubt after Mavericks hire Masai Ujiri — Jason Kidd wanted to be the team president of the Dallas Mavericks. Now the Mavs have hired Masai Ujiri, and the new president has stopped short of endorsing Kidd as his long-term head coach.

items.blurb: Pistons already showing their place in playoff power ranking is wrong — The Detroit Pistons proved their success isn't a flash in the pan with a strong 111-101 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers on Tuesday in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.blurb: Knicks mop up 76ers inside in tight Game 2 win — The New York Knicks outscored the Philadelphia 76ers in the paint, 56-30, on their way to a 108-102 victory.

items.blurb: Thunder emerging as stronger favorites against Lakers, as Luka Doncic's status remains uncertain — Doncic hasn't played since April 2 because of a Grade 2 left hamstring strain.

items.blurb: Michael Reinsdorf Apologizes To Bulls Fans As Bryson Graham Signals Full Rebuild — Chicago Bulls CEO Michael Reinsdorf publicly apologized to the franchise's fanbase Wednesday and accepted personal responsibility for the organization's prolonged struggles, speaking alongside Bryson Graham, the newly hired vice president of basketball operations, at an introductory news conference. items.headline: Brunson powers Knicks to 2-0 series lead over 76ers
items.blurb: Brunson powers Knicks to 2-0 series lead over 76ers — Jalen Brunson scored 26 points and helped New York seize control of a close game after a run of blowouts, leading the Knicks to a 108-102 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers on Wednesday night for a 2-0 lead in the Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.blurb: Bucks Co-Owner Jimmy Haslam Wants Giannis Antetokounmpo's Future Resolved Before NBA Draft — Milwaukee Bucks co-owner Jimmy Haslam said Wednesday the franchise hopes to resolve Giannis Antetokounmpo's future before next month's NBA draft, framing the decision as one that will shape the organization's entire roster construction approach this offseason. items.blurb: Jaylen Brown Says He Wants To Play In Boston For Next 10 Years, Explains 'Favorite Season' Comments — Jaylen Brown directly addressed speculation about his future with the Boston Celtics on Wednesday night, declaring his desire to remain with the franchise long term and pushing back against reports of organizational tension. items.blurb: Nick Nurse rejoins 76ers after brother’s funeral, says he would want him to coach — Nick Nurse rejoined the Philadelphia 76ers after attending his brother’s funeral, saying Wednesday that Steve Nurse was a fan who would want him at Game 2 of the Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.headline: Lakers' Luka Doncic reveals timeline for injury recovery
items.blurb: Lakers' Luka Doncic reveals timeline for injury recovery — The Los Angeles Lakers and Oklahoma City Thunder played on Tuesday in Game 1 of the Western Conference semifinals. Without star guard Luka Doncic, the Lakers just couldn't overcome the Thunder, falling 108-90.

items.blurb: Christian Braun Takes Accountability For Poor Playoffs Amid Undisclosed Injury — Christian Braun played through a severe left ankle sprain and an undisclosed left calf injury during the Denver Nuggets' first-round playoff loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, contributing to a physically compromised and statistically underwhelming performance that has raised questions about his future with the franc

items.blurb: Jared McCain 'Definitely Hurt' By 'Sold High' Comment After Trade From Sixers — Jared McCain's arrival with the Oklahoma City reflects the kind of calculated, forward-looking roster construction that has defined Sam Presti's tenure. items.blurb: Opting for continuity, Raptors expected to offer extensions to Webster, Rajakovic — It’s expected that both general manager Bobby Webster and head coach Darko Rajakovic will be offered contract extensions in the coming weeks, Michael Grange has learned. He breaks it down here.

items.blurb: Luka Doncic Reveals PRP Treatment In Spain, Remains Day-To-Day With Grade 2 Hamstring Strain — Los Angeles Lakers star Luka Doncic offered his first public comments since suffering a Grade 2 hamstring strain on April 2, telling reporters Wednesday he has no firm return date and remains frustrated watching his teammates compete in the playoffs without him. items.headline: Joel Embiid Out For Game 2 With Ankle, Hip Injuries
items.blurb: Joel Embiid Out For Game 2 With Ankle, Hip Injuries — Joel Embiid has been ruled out of Game 2 against the New York Knicks by the Philadelphia 76ers due to ankle and hip injuries. Embiid was unable to participate in shootaround on Wednesday morning after experiencing soreness in both his ankle and hip. Embiid has been receiving around-the-clock treatments in hopes of playing Game 2. The Sixers ent

items.headline: Brad Stevens Pushes Back On Reports Of Jaylen Brown Rift With Celtics
items.blurb: Brad Stevens Pushes Back On Reports Of Jaylen Brown Rift With Celtics — Brad Stevens dismissed reports of tension between Jaylen Brown and the Boston Celtics Wednesday, saying a recent direct conversation with the All-NBA wing produced nothing but positive dialogue, four days after Boston's stunning first-round playoff exit against the Philadelphia 76ers. items.headline: Raptors GM Webster reflects on 2025-26 season, weighs in on off-season plans
items.blurb: Raptors GM Webster reflects on 2025-26 season, weighs in on off-season plans — Watch the entire year-end press conference from Toronto Raptors GM Bobby Webster, looking back on a good season of growth, a 7-game first round playoff series, and also touches on summer plans.

items.headline: Why Raptors’ Webster may not deal with Ujiri: ‘He knows the players we like’
items.blurb: Why Raptors’ Webster may not deal with Ujiri: ‘He knows the players we like’ — Toronto Raptors general manager Bobby Webster speaks on Masai Ujiri’s new role with the Dallas Mavericks, saying that it might be hard for him to make deals with his former boss since they know each other so well.

items.headline: Webster, Raptors’ off-season plan is simple: ‘More, better players’
items.blurb: Webster, Raptors’ off-season plan is simple: ‘More, better players’ — Toronto Raptors GM Bobby Webster discusses the team’s outlook heading into the offseason, saying that dipping into the luxury tax is always a possibility for a contending squad, but that the Raptors are still looking to bolster their overall talent.

items.blurb: How does Raptors’ front office navigate off-season roster questions? — NBA front office insider Bobby Marks joins The JD Bunkis Podcast and delves into the Toronto Raptors’ roster and the different directions that the front office can go to improve the team, including through trade.

items.headline: Masai Ujiri Noncommittal On Jason Kidd's Future, Eager To Build Around Cooper Flagg
items.blurb: Masai Ujiri Noncommittal On Jason Kidd's Future, Eager To Build Around Cooper Flagg — Masai Ujiri, named president of basketball operations by the Dallas Mavericks on Monday, declined to commit to coach Jason Kidd's future with the franchise while expressing enthusiasm about building around reigning Rookie of the Year Cooper Flagg . items.headline: Warriors linked to new potential Steve Kerr replacement
items.blurb: Warriors linked to new potential Steve Kerr replacement — Kerr's future with the franchise remains unclear, and the team has been linked with a new potential replacement for the four-time champion coach.

items.headline: Julius Randle: Cools off in loss
items.blurb: Julius Randle: Cools off in loss — Randle produced 12 points (4-10 FG, 0-2 3Pt, 4-5 FT), five rebounds, two assists and two steals in 27 minutes during Wednesday's 133-95 loss to the Spurs in Game 2 of the Western Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama: Another Strong showing Wednesday
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama: Another Strong showing Wednesday — Wembanyama finished with 19 points (7-15 FG, 2-7 3Pt, 3-3 FT), 15 rebounds, two assists, one steal and two blocks across 26 minutes in Wednesday's 133-95 win over Minnesota in Game 2 of the Western Conference Semifinals. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Pacers Hopeful Of Retaining Draft Pick But Don't Regret Ivica Zubac Trade
items.blurb: Pacers Hopeful Of Retaining Draft Pick But Don't Regret Ivica Zubac Trade — The Indiana Pacers have a lot on the line in Sunday's 2026 NBA Draft Lottery. If the Pacers pick falls between No. 1 and No. 4, they'll keep their pick. If that pick falls at No. 5 or No. 6 the pick will go to the LA Clippers. items.blurb: Michael Porter Jr Wants To Stay With Nets; Sees Good Future In Brooklyn — Brooklyn Nets forward Michael Porter Jr. admits that he was frustrated by being in a losing situation for the first time in his career.
